items.title: Trans-Fettsäuren in der Ernährung bei Endometriose
items.visual_angle: editorial still life with healthy ingredients, hands, table scene, soft lifestyle composition
items.prompt:
Create a website header image for the blog title: "Trans-Fettsäuren in der Ernährung bei Endometriose".

Brand anchor:
A calm digital-health editorial style for women's health: soft pastel palette, clean backgrounds, subtle texture, gentle natural light, compassionate modern healthcare mood, premium but approachable, minimal clutter, visually consistent across a whole blog series.

Required series consistency:
- Same overall art direction as the rest of the series
- Palette alignment: blush #EFC8D3, rose #D89AAF, plum #7C4A5B, lavender #C9C2E8, teal #6FB7B2, cream #F8F4EF, charcoal #2F2A2E
- Principles: empathetic, calm, editorial, clean health-tech, minimal, hopeful, non-sensational
- Composition: soft editorial composition with one clear focal subject and minimal background clutter
- Safe zone: leave clean negative space on the right third for title overlay
- Topic angle: editorial still life with healthy ingredients, hands, table scene, soft lifestyle composition

Content guidance:
- Interpret the title metaphorically and editorially, not literally
- Keep the subject matter medically respectful and emotionally safe
- Favor hope, clarity, self-efficacy, and trustworthy health communication
- Suitable for a women's health article header on a premium digital-health website
- No embedded text
- No logos or watermarks
- No collage look
- No stock-photo cliché expressions
- Use gentle natural light and a soft pastel color grade
- Keep enough clean negative space for a future headline overlay
items.negative_prompt: avoid photorealistic medical gore; avoid hospital drama aesthetics; avoid fear-based imagery; avoid text, letters, logos and watermarks inside the image; prefer soft daylight, pastel surfaces, calm body language; use consistent lens feel and color grading across the full series; show women-centered, supportive, modern healthcare context where relevant; keep images suitable for website hero/header use
